danger_signals.visual_form: Rhythmic patterns of oncoming enemy sprites in screen-filling arrangements
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat intensity; player must dodge and counterattack

danger_signals.visual_form: Bizarre, exaggerated biological and mechanical hybrid creatures with muscle-bound aesthetics
danger_signals.meaning: Visual communication of 'otherness' and hostile intent through camp horror imagery

danger_signals.visual_form: Dense bullet patterns filling screen space
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger requiring pattern recognition and evasion

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible sprites dropped by defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Reward for successful engagement; progression toward greater capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual feedback from player character transformations and enhanced attack states
safety_signals.meaning: Player has offensive capacity and temporary invulnerability windows

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Muscular male bodies, warrior-aesthetic designs with exaggerated physiques
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Heroes depicted as heroic, hyper-masculine figures with distinctive long spiked hair (Idaten) and companion cherubs (Benten)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-controlled sprites; occupies safe/active screen space during gameplay

faction_indicators.faction: Antagonist forces (Bo Emperor/Su Emperor Roid and Neo Builder Army)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Grotesque, exaggerated body-conscious and steroid-themed enemy designs; mechanical and organic hybrids
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bo Empress Conscious and Su Emperor Roid serve as visual embodiments of corporate/medical body modification perversion
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy sprites; waves of incoming threats; occupies hostile screen space

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy variety increases; sprite complexity and visual grotesqueness intensifies; screen fills with more elaborate formations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy AI becomes more aggressive; attack patterns become more intricate and demanding; difficulty curve manifests through visual density

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bright weapon effects; explosive feedback from player attacks; visual feedback of enemy destruction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player character performs enhanced actions; enemies visibly collapse or explode; screen-clearing capabilities available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vivid, wacky surreal imagery; exaggerated musculature on all humanoid entities; camp aesthetic throughout visual design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters perform exaggerated attack animations (e.g., gyrating, flatulating); interactions carry comedic, over-the-top energy

environmental_storytelling.example: Idaten's long spiked hair and warrior body communicates his role as heroic protagonist; Samson/Adon's gyrating attacks visually communicate their unconventional power source and comedic role
Bo Empress Conscious and Su Emperor Roid's pun-based design (body-conscious, steroid references) communicates the game's satirical stance on body culture and enhancement through visual grotesquerie

Surreal, impossible imagery (moon-riding Adam, battleship-girl hybrid Mami 19, pagoda-ship Sabu) establishes world as divorced from reality; communicates 'baka-ge' (idiot game) aesthetic through visual abundance
How characters move and attack (exaggerated poses, flatulation, gyration) communicates personality and faction identity without dialogue; establishes camp-homoerotic tone visually

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Performs varied body-based attacks; visible as primary agent of destruction against enemy waves
Flanked by cherub companions that transform into 'mini-hunks'; attack patterns incorporate companion mechanics
Advance in rhythmic, patterned waves; projectiles fill screen; visual presence communicates intensity and danger
Visually disappear/explode with feedback effects; drop power-ups; screen progressively clears as threat diminishes

notes: Cho Aniki communicates world-state primarily through visual excess and surreal aesthetic rather than subtle environmental cues. The game's manifestation layer relies on: (1) character design as faction identifier and narrative communicator; (2) enemy visual grotesquerie as embodiment of thematic opposition (body modification/steroid culture as evil); (3) exaggerated animation as tone-setter (camp, homoerotic, comedic); (4) screen density and projectile patterns as danger/threat visualization. The SNES version maintains the core homoerotic, wacky surreal aesthetic that defines the series. Political/faction communication is implicit in the pun-based enemy designs (Bo Emperor/Su Emperor Roid satirizing body consciousness and steroid culture). World-state transitions from equilibrium to escalating threat are communicated entirely through visual density, enemy complexity, and animation intensity rather than narrative exposition.
